verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (hK2) is a secreted serine protease of the kallikrein family. It is synthesized as a preproprotein, processed, and secreted into the extracellular space/prostatic fluid. It lacks a transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or any documented stable surface-anchoring mechanism. Its detection in serum/prostatic fluid reflects a freely secreted form. No isoform is membrane-anchored, and no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering has been documented. It is not targeted by cell-surface-directed antibody or CAR-T programs against the protein body on cells.
